Event: Barney drinks alcohol despite it destroying him
Explanation: Habitual drinking wrecks his own life and contributes little or nothing to others.

Event: Barney gives up alcohol temporarily and becomes addicted to coffee
Explanation: He gains some functioning via sobriety but replaces it with another dependency that Moe exploits.

Event: Barney lives a dysfunctional life brought on by alcoholism
Explanation: His ongoing alcoholic lifestyle keeps him in a chronically damaged state with no offsetting gain.

Event: Barney licks beer off the floor while claiming he still has dignity
Explanation: Degrading behavior for minimal short-term gratification further harms his dignity and health.

Event: Barney tries to fight his alcoholism
Explanation: His efforts at sobriety significantly improve his capabilities and reduce collateral damage.

Event: Barney kicks the bottle and excels in astronaut training
Explanation: Sobriety unlocks his genius, benefits his career prospects and serves the program well.

Event: Barney is okay with being touched in the shelter after learning it was Skinner
Explanation: He drops an unnecessary objection and accepts harmless contact, easing tension.

Event: Barney happily becomes “the wife” in a cult marriage to Otto
Explanation: He participates willingly in a mutual, if odd, arrangement with no clear harm shown.

Event: Barney admits he is in love with Homer
Explanation: Openly stating his feelings is honest, costs him nothing clearly, and can deepen their bond.

Event: Barney gives up drinking in later seasons (attempted relevance)
Explanation: Sobriety improves his life trajectory and reduces burdens on those around him.

Event: Barney becomes enraged when his beer is ruined or blocked
Explanation: Overreaction to loss of alcohol harms his relations and self-control for no real gain.

Event: Barney knocks Homer out with one punch when drunk
Explanation: He shows effective fighting ability but harms a friend and risks consequences.

Event: Barney knocks out Wade Boggs and Moe while drunk
Explanation: His combat skill empowers him but causes physical harm and likely social fallout.

Event: Barney easily produces the best film in Springfield’s festival
Explanation: His artistry brings him recognition while deeply moving others.

Event: Barney crushes Homer’s plow business as the Plow King
Explanation: His business success benefits him but directly damages Homer’s livelihood.

Event: Barney proves the most talented singer in the Be Sharps
Explanation: His singing boosts his own success and the group’s appeal.

Event: Barney completes astronaut training without a hitch (sober)
Explanation: He gains a prestigious qualification and becomes a reliable asset to the mission.

Event: Barney doesn’t care much that alcohol destroys his abilities
Explanation: Indifference to a self-ruining flaw keeps him trapped with little compensating benefit.

Event: Barney stands in line at unemployment agency seeking work
Explanation: Attempting to get a job is a rational move toward stability and reduces reliance on others.

Event: Barney donates sperm, producing many children
Explanation: His donations likely provide him money and give many families children.

Event: Barney takes Bart’s skateboard and breaks it in half
Explanation: He vents frustration by destroying Bart’s property, gaining minor emotional relief while harming Bart.

Event: Barney sobers up for a few seasons
Explanation: Sustained sobriety significantly improves his functioning and reduces harm to others.

Event: Moe starts serving Barney coffee to keep profiting from his addiction
Explanation: Moe gains steady revenue while perpetuating Barney’s dependency.

Event: Barney goes streaking at his high school senior prom
Explanation: He risks humiliation for a brief thrill that mildly entertains others more than it helps him.

Event: Barney works as a male stripper during economic hardship
Explanation: He finds income in a downturn and provides entertainment, with no added downside shown.

Event: Barney decides to give up alcohol after learning friends see him as a “tanked up loser”
Explanation: Recognition of stigma motivates self-improvement that benefits him and eases friends’ discomfort.

Event: Barney’s friends at Moe’s try to make Homer the new Barney
Explanation: They seek someone to normalize their drinking, at Homer’s expense.

Event: Barney charges a tab to Mr. Burns
Explanation: He gets drinks on credit with a dangerous creditor, gaining in the short term but risking serious fallout.

Event: Barney considers the painful consequences “worth it” after the Burns tab incident
Explanation: His stated valuation encourages repeating self-harming behavior for trivial gain.

Event: Barney lets his apartment become such a mess he can’t find his dog
Explanation: Extreme neglect harms his own living conditions and his pet with no benefit.

Event: Barney pulls out a tranquilizer dart and drinks the remaining liquid
Explanation: He immediately consumes an unknown drug, severely risking health for negligible extra buzz.

Event: Barney switches addiction from alcohol to coffee when sober
Explanation: He reduces alcohol damage but maintains a self-limiting dependency that slightly benefits others (e.g., Moe).

Event: Barney drinks non-alcoholic champagne in celebration, undoing his sobriety
Explanation: He impulsively drinks a risky celebratory beverage and loses all his hard-earned progress.

Event: Homer introduces teen Barney to beer while he’s studying for SATs
Explanation: Homer gains a drinking buddy while derailing Barney’s Harvard-bound path.

Event: Barney starts drinking more after girlfriend Chloe leaves for her career
Explanation: He escalates a destructive coping mechanism that harms him with no clear upside.

Event: Homer sneaks out of work and goes to Duff Brewery with Barney
Explanation: Homer and Barney enjoy free samples at the cost of work responsibility and potential safety.

Event: Barney consumes more free beer samples than Homer at the brewery
Explanation: He maximizes personal intoxication, worsening his own impairment without helping anyone.

Event: Homer drives home as designated driver with a drunk Barney
Explanation: Homer’s relative sobriety protects Barney and others despite his own minor risk.

Event: Barney tells the cops to use the breathalyzer on Homer
Explanation: He deflects attention onto Homer to save himself, leading to Homer’s license suspension.

Event: Cops arrest Homer and suspend his license while letting Barney go
Explanation: Stupid
column8: They punish the less-drunk driver and free the more dangerous one, increasing future risk.

Event: Barney drives home drunk after Homer’s arrest and crashes into Wiggum’s car
Explanation: He endangers himself and others for no gain and immediately causes property damage.

Event: Barney repeatedly donates sperm to the IVF clinic
Explanation: He gains ongoing compensation and helps many people have children.

Event: Barney gets so drunk he forgets major events like his birthday party and his acclaimed film
Explanation: Blackout-level drinking erases his own achievements and memories without benefiting anyone.
